meta.purpose: Terminology boundaries, traps, and self-checks for GSAP Master.
meta.load_condition: Load with Core for any generation, review, or refactor task in this domain.

standard_terms.definition: The repeatable rules of movement that make a product or video feel coherent.
The order in which the viewer's eye is guided through the scene.
The change of speed over time that gives motion weight, intent, and emotional tone.
Mapping animation progress directly to scroll position.

banned_terms.why: It gives no intent, audience, hierarchy, or success criteria.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Motion without hierarchy becomes noise and reduces perceived quality.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Too vague unless translated into concrete constraints: restraint, rhythm, typography, spacing, and material behavior.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Some interactions are better solved with CSS, native transitions, or no animation.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

misunderstandings.correct: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: World-class motion often removes movement to increase clarity, weight, and trust.
applies_when: Designing a corporate landing page where key value propositions need to stand out without visual noise
Animating form validation states or dashboard data updates where focus and accuracy matter
Creating micro-interactions for buttons or toggles that need to feel responsive and grounded
Refining a hero section transition to prevent user disorientation during navigation
does_not_apply_when: Developing a gaming UI where constant motion is the core experience and removing it would make the interface feel broken
Creating children's educational onboarding where exaggerated motion is required to guide attention and removing it would confuse users
Designing a high-energy event countdown where rapid animations set the mood and minimizing movement would kill the intended vibe
Building a physics simulation demo where continuous motion is the data itself and removing it would hide the system behavior
misunderstandings.failure_risk: The agent will generate cluttered distracting animations that bury key UI elements and reduce user trust in the product professionalism.

misunderstandings.wrong: Animation quality starts with library choice.
misunderstandings.correct: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: Library choice matters after attention path, timing, intent, and information hierarchy are defined.
applies_when: Planning an animation system for a SaaS dashboard before writing any tween code
Drafting a storyboarding phase for a product marketing video or scroll-driven narrative
Deciding between GSAP ScrollTrigger CSS animations or Framer Motion for a specific UI transition
Conducting a code review where a developer prioritized complex easing functions over clear visual hierarchy
does_not_apply_when: Integrating animations into a legacy codebase with strict bundle limits where tool constraints must dictate the approach first
Building a WebGL particle system where performance profiling and library capabilities must drive the initial architecture
Following a corporate design system that mandates a specific animation tool where deviating to plan timing first would violate engineering compliance
Prototyping a rapid UI proof-of-concept where speed of implementation with a chosen library outweighs detailed timing planning
misunderstandings.failure_risk: The agent will produce technically complex but visually incoherent animations that fail to guide the user or communicate the intended message.

self_check: Can a first-time viewer identify the main visual subject within one second?
Does every meaningful motion have a reason beyond decoration?
If 30% of the motion were removed, would the communication become clearer rather than weaker?
